title: Norpro, Red Silicone Jar Opener, 6"
description:
Easily open jars and bottles
Features a secure non-slip grip that allows you to effortlessly remove lids
Also perfect for use as a potholder or trivet
Measures 6in/15cm in diameter
Dishwasher safe
reviews_5s:
We use it for opening jars and under slippery plates when cutting a roast so the plate doesn’t move. Also we use it for a trivet. It really didn’t have much of a smell. The thickness is good. Any thicker and you won’t be able to use it. It is smooth on one side and has grips on the other. I am not sure if grips would be best on both sides or not. Good overall.I've had something similar in the house for years - not silicone, rubber. It works great so I thought this might do the trick with this newer gas can I have in the barn. I have pretty bad hands and I've read this new generation of gas cans is hard for everyone, but especially someone with arthritic hands. Well it works I'm happy to say. I am also going to upgrade with another one for the house.This jar opener comes in very handy. It’s big enough to cover many of the larger jar lids. It’s got some thickness to it, it’s not flimsy like some of those other ones.Really helps to open jars, IF jar is not super large - as long your hand does fit comfortably on lid it’ll work.This jar opener does what it is supposed to do and is very durable. It looks brand new even after using several times.Works great!Works!The opener is very goodI love this product. I have four of these jar openers, and I use them for a variety of purposes. I use them to anchor a jar on the counter so I can open it more easily. I often put one of the silicon jar openers flat on a saucer, and put a bowl of soup on it. That way I don't have to worry when I take my hot liquid soup into the dining room that it will spill. I really like this product.If you sometimes struggle with opening jars, this little do-hicky may make life easier. I have an assortment of jar openers, but this one is a definite improvement: just a little larger and "sticky" so it grips the jar lid.
It also arrived very fast. So. . . a "win-win" all around.
reviews_1s: My grip strength is decent, not great, but no match for today's silicone lined jar lids. This just slides around the lid, useless. I will see if I can find another use for it.I DO NOT RECOMMEND THIS PRODUCT IT JUST SLIPS ON THE LID WHEN TURNING. A WASTE OF MONEY!Products should allow me to open jars, right? Nope! Will be returning.This doesn't work for a grip because it slips. So I will have to just use it for a trivet. I bought another brand that does the job.Slipped when I tried to use itTried it on 2 glass jars that were vacuum sealed: pickles and sauerkraut. Forget about it. The old standby: using a flat head utensil knife works just fine. The trick is finding the access on the lid edge for the knife to slip under. Once done, tilt the knife to pop the vacuum and voila! Open sesame! Oh well, another "to good to be true" product, bites the dust.This is awful. Makes your grip WORSE. Slips like crazy. No leverage. The 4 starts are a complete lie. I threw it away.Doesn’t work.Doesn’t work. At all. But I bought it bc my mom had one from the old school and it worked. Guess they don’t make them like they used to.I am an elderly blind woman and I purchased this to assist me in opening bottles jars etc. what a waste of money! It doesn’t work at all! Shame on you, shame on you, shame on you for creating this trash!
